title: Read clone/proxy immutable arguments from bytecode using extcodecopy
trigger_patterns: read immutable args from clone bytecode
description: ERC6551 clones (and other minimal-proxy patterns) append immutable arguments at the tail of the runtime bytecode. extcodecopy(address, destOffset, codeOffset, len) copies these tail bytes into memory cheaply — far cheaper than a storage read. For ERC6551, the layout at offset 0x4d is (chainId, tokenContract, tokenId), 96 bytes total. No SLOAD, no constructor parameter, just a bytecode read.
when_to_apply: Reading immutable args from minimal clones (ERC6551, LibClone) where the arguments are appended to the runtime bytecode
when_not_to_apply: Regular contracts where immutables are baked into bytecode (use immutable keyword), or contracts that aren't deployed as appended-arg clones
